text:
In the heart of the Whispering Woods lived a solitary deer named Thumper. Whenever loneliness crept in, Thumper would gaze at the sparkling pond below the dense canopy. It's there he'd imagine spending forever, reflecting on life's simple pleasures—like the way the sun dappled through the leaves and the gentle rustling of the night birds.

One day, Thumper noticed something strange. Birds weren't singing; there was no singing critter in sight. Panicking a little, Thumper approached the edge. A wise old woodpecker paused mid-cavity search.

"Ah," the woodpecker said as it rested its bill. "I've heard whisperings like that before. You've caught my feathered friend on a rare birding trip."

* * *

The woodpecker regaled Thumper with tales of brilliant migrations and hidden nests. Thumper enjoyed listening, finding comfort in the beauty he knew these travelers couldn't see.
